With all due respect,

Many Iranian students have been living and studying in the UAE for years. Through hard work, dedication, and support from their schools, we have built our education and our futures here. For many of us, this country is not just where we study – it has become our second home.

Many students and families came here seeking a safe, stable, and peaceful place to learn and grow. Please do not take away this opportunity by closing Iranian schools, which allow us to continue our education in our own language while fully respecting UAE laws and culture.

Education is not political; it is the foundation of our future. Please help us continue our studies and preserve hope for Iranian students living in the UAE.

Thank you very much for your attention, fairness, and support.

Many Iranians have lived in the UAE for years. Through hard work, dedication, and investment, they have built their lives and futures here. For many of us, this place is not just where we work — it has become our second home.

Please do not bring ordinary people into politics. Ordinary people are not responsible for political decisions.Please do not take away the hope of the Iranian people who are living here.

Dear Your Highness Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum,
My name is Aaryan Rabari. I am currently living in Dubai and I have started a personal mission called “The 1 Million Plastic Bottle Recycling Challenge”.
My goal is to collect and recycle 1 million plastic bottles to help protect the environment and keep Dubai clean. Every day I collect empty plastic bottles from different places like restaurants, public areas, and streets.
This mission is not easy, but I believe small actions can make a big difference for our planet. I also share my daily progress on my YouTube channel to inspire more people to recycle and care about the environment.
It would be a great honor for me if you could support or encourage this mission. Your inspiration can help motivate many people in Dubai to participate in recycling and environmental protection.
Thank you for your time and for inspiring the youth of Dubai.
Kind regards,
Aaryan Rabari
Dubai, UAE
“The 1 Million Bottle Mission”

📖 Surah Al-Hashr – Verse 14
“They will not fight you all together except within fortified cities or from behind walls. Their hostility among themselves is severe. You think they are united, but their hearts are divided. That is because they are a people who do not reason.”

Explanation (Tafsir)
This verse was revealed in the context of conflict between the Muslims of Madinah and certain hostile groups at that time.
“They will not fight you all together except within fortified cities or from behind walls”
This means they lack the courage and unity to confront openly. They prefer protection — hiding in strongholds or fighting defensively rather than facing directly.
“Their hostility among themselves is severe”
Although they may appear united against a common opponent, internally they have deep اختلاف (conflicts), jealousy, and mistrust among themselves.
“You think they are united, but their hearts are divided”
Outwardly, they may look organized and strong. But inwardly they are divided in intentions, beliefs, and loyalties. Their unity is only superficial.
“That is because they are a people who do not reason”
Their division and weakness come from a failure to truly understand truth, justice, and wisdom.

(اللهم إني أسألك بأسمائك الحسنى كلها، وأسألك باسمك العظيم الأعظم الأعزّ الأجلّ الأكرم أن تستجيب لي):

اللهم أنت 【الملك】: ارزقني في الجنة ملكا لا ينفد•
‏‎اللهم أنت 【السلام】: أسألك السلامة في ديني ودنياي وآخرتي • 
‏‎اللهم أنت 【المُؤَمِّن】: أمنّي فزع يوم القيامة •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【العزيز】: أسألك العزة في الدنيا والآخرة •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【السميع】: اجعلني أستمع لأفضل القول وأتبّع أحسنه •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【المصوّر】: صوّر لي نعيم الجنة عند الموت •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الرؤوف】: ارأف لحالي وتجاوز عن سيئاتي •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الحسيب】: أدخلني الجنة بدون حساب أو عقاب •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الباعث】: ابعثني على الإيمان بك، وبكتابك، وبرسولك ﷺ •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الغني】: أغنني بفضلك عمن سواك •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الجميل】: جمّلني بعظيم سترك في الدنيا والآخرة •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【التواب】: أسألك توبة نصوح إلى يوم أن ألقاك •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الحق】: اجعلني من أهل الحق والاستقامة •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【اللطيف】: الطف بي بلطفك الخفي •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【المحيي】: احيي قلبي بذكرك •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الكريم】: أكرمني بلقائك على أفضل حال ترضاه لأحد من أوليائك •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الرزّاق】: ارزقني من حيث أحتسب ومن حيث لا أحتسب •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الجامع】: اجمعني وأهلي وذريتي برسولك الكريم في الجنة •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【النافع】: انفعني بالذكر والقرآن •
‏‎اللهم أنت【 القوي】: قوّني على الصلاة والصيام والقيام •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【العدل】: أسألك العدل في النطق والفعل والعمل •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【القادر】: قدّر لي حُسن الخاتمة •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الغفور】: اغفر لي ولأهلي وللمسلمين جميعاً مغفرةً عامة ً تامّةً •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الأول】: احشرني في زمرة الأولين المتقين •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【العليّ】: أسألك العلوّ في الجنة والفردوس •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الشكور】: ألهمني شكرك ليل نهار •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الرحيم】: ارحم ضعفي وذليّ وقلّة حيلتي •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الرافع】: ارفع ذكري في السماوات والأرض •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【القابض】: اقبض نفسي على كلمة لا إله إلا اللّه •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الهادي】: اهدني إلى صراطك المستقيم •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الولي】: اجعلني من أوليائك الصالحين•
‏‎اللهم أنت 【المعين】: أعنّي على طاعتك وجنّبني معصيتك •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الودود】: ارزقني ودّك، و ودّ نبيك، وعبادك الصالحين
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الجليل】: اكسني من جلالك يوم ألقاك •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【العظيم】: عظّم محبتك، ومحبة نبيك الكريم في قلبي •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الوارث】: اجعلني وأهلي من ورثة جنّة النعيم •
‏‎اللهم أنت 【الحميد】: املأ قلبي حمداً وشكراً لك .

تحية ملؤها الاحترام والتقدير لشخصكم الكريم ولأمكم الغالية، سمو الشيخة هند بنت مكتوم.
 إن البر والوفاء الذي تظهره لوالدتك الكريمة، وخاصة في ذكرى ميلادها في ١٢ فبراير، هو أنبل مثال للحب الطاهر والقيم الإسلامية الأصيلة.
 أنتم رمزٌ للجود وجمال الروح في هذا العالم.

 هذه المدينة الإسلامية العظيمة التي تفيض بالأمن والأمان تحت ظل قيادتكم الحكيمة.
 فهل يتكرم سموكم، تقديراً لهذه المحبة المقدسة بينكم وبين والدتكم، بمنحي فرصة للعيش في كنف دبي؟
 ستكون هذه المكرمة أغلى هدية لي في حياتي.

A beautiful family iftar in 2026 in Dubai begins long before the fast is actually broken — with anticipation, preparation, and heartfelt excitement.
The sun slowly sets behind the glittering skyline, and the Burj Khalifa glows in golden evening light. As the call to prayer echoes through the air, everyone pauses. It’s time.
Inside a spacious majlis or a warmly decorated garden, the whole family gathers together. The grandparents sit proudly at the head, grateful and smiling. Uncles and aunts pass around dates and water. Brothers and sisters whisper and laugh. Cousins take photos. The children, dressed in their best outfits, move around with bright, excited eyes.
The table is filled with delicious traditional dishes: sweet dates, laban, warm lentil soup, fresh khubz bread, crispy samosas, stuffed grape leaves, fragrant machboos with tender lamb, grilled chicken, creamy hummus, and golden kunafa topped with pistachios. The air carries the comforting scent of cardamom and saffron.
When everyone breaks their fast together with the first date, there is a brief, beautiful silence — a shared moment of gratitude, faith, and togetherness. Then the warmth begins to grow louder: plates are passed around, everyone insists others eat more, stories are told, jokes are shared, and laughter fills the space.
Later, the adults sit together enjoying Arabic coffee and tea, while the children play outside under the soft night sky. Perhaps the family takes a peaceful walk along the illuminated Dubai Marina or visits a Ramadan night gathering near the Jumeirah Mosque.
What makes this iftar truly beautiful is not just the food or the city — it’s the love. Multiple generations under one roof, united by faith, tradition, and deep family bonds. In that moment, everyone feels it clearly: this is home.

The magic of the first weekend in Ramadan in Dubai 2026 reveals itself in many quiet, yet powerful moments. The city slips into a gentle stillness as the sun slowly hides behind the skyscrapers, and the skyline bathes in golden hues. The air carries the scent of fresh pastries, spices, and the sound of fountains being cooled by the evening breeze. In street cafés, you hear soft conversations, family laughter, and a calm anticipation for the nightly iftar.
In residential areas, homes glow brighter, gardens shimmer with string lights, and mosques around the world call the faithful to prayer at designated times. The city offers a sense of community: neighbors share drinks and dates generously, while friends and strangers greet each other with warmth and welcome. The first Taraweeh prayers in mosques and converted venues fill the air with a gentle spirituality that touches heart and soul. It is a time for reflection, gratitude, and hope—an moment where ritual and modern life seem to harmonize.
Kindness flows through small gestures: a door opened, a message of support offered, a quiet blessing spoken for family and friends. Megacities like Dubai show how tradition and modernity grow together—lights illuminating the night, encounters that bring warmth.
May this Ramadan be energetic and peaceful, blessed for all who observe it. May health, patience, gratitude, and love shape your days. May Allah’s blessing accompany you, grant you strength, and strengthen your community. Blessings to you, your family, and all those around you who celebrate this holy month. Ramadan Kareem.
